title: ILI9341 2.4-inch TFT LCD — SPI Display Interface
content: The ILI9341 is a 240×320 pixel 16-bit colour TFT LCD controller used in 2.4–3.2 inch displays. It communicates via 4-wire SPI at up to 80 MHz. The display supports 262K colours (18-bit) but most Arduino libraries use 65K (16-bit RGB565). A touchscreen (XPT2046 resistive controller) is often integrated on the same breakout.
extended_content: Wiring: CS, DC (data/command), RST, MOSI (SDA), SCK — MISO optional (read-back). Typical library: Adafruit ILI9341 + Adafruit GFX; TFT_eSPI (ESP32, configurable SPI clock to 80 MHz). Pixel format RGB565: 5 bits red, 6 bits green, 5 bits blue packed into uint16_t. Filling a 320×240 screen requires sending 153,600 bytes; at 40 MHz SPI (3.2 µs per byte) takes ~0.5 s — partial-screen updates and double buffering are essential for animation. Backlight on PWM pin (BL); 10–30 mA at 3.3 V, controlled for brightness. Touch: XPT2046 on a separate SPI CS pin; 12-bit ADC reports stylus X/Y in raw units (calibration: map raw to pixel coords with two-point linear fit). VCC: 3.3 V for LCD and touch (use level shifter for 5 V MCU SPI). Applications: oscilloscope display, GUI for data loggers, game display (Arduino GFX), graphical instrument cluster. Faster alternative: ST7789 (no CS pin, but 240×240 / 240×320 and faster SPI).
units: time: s, SPI_clock: Hz, pixels: count
source: ILI Technology ILI9341 Datasheet V1.11; Adafruit ILI9341 Library Documentation; TFT_eSPI Library Wiki
